skill.name: Azure Event Hubs SDK for Rust
skill.description: Rust SDK for Azure Event Hubs - stream and ingest events with partitioned processing and producer-consumer patterns
skill.summary: Official Rust client library for Azure Event Hubs enabling scalable event ingestion and streaming data pipelines
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill contains documentation-only content for the Azure Event Hubs Rust SDK. No executable code, scripts, or dangerous patterns detected. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 risk factors. The skill provides guidance on using Azure's official Rust client library for event streaming.

content.user_title: Stream Events with Azure Event Hubs in Rust
content.value_statement: Building event-driven applications in Rust requires reliable event ingestion. This skill provides official Azure Event Hubs SDK patterns for producers and consumers.
actual_capabilities: Create ProducerClient to send events to Azure Event Hubs
Create ConsumerClient to receive events from specific partitions
Send single events or batch multiple events efficiently
Query event hub properties including partition IDs and sequence numbers
Implement checkpointing with Azure Blob storage for distributed consumers
Use Azure Developer Tools Credential for authentication
limitations: Requires Azure subscription and Event Hubs namespace configuration
Environment variables must be set for namespace host and event hub name
Does not cover advanced features like event processors or load balancing

use_cases.title: Real-time Data Ingestion Pipeline
use_cases.description: Build scalable event producers to ingest streaming data from IoT devices or application logs into Azure Event Hubs for downstream processing

use_cases.title: Event-Driven Microservices
use_cases.description: Implement consumer clients to process events from specific partitions enabling parallel consumption across microservice instances

use_cases.title: Telemetry Collection System
use_cases.description: Deploy Rust-based event collectors that batch and send telemetry data efficiently with proper error handling and retry logic

prompt_templates.title: Basic Event Producer
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Rust function that uses the Azure Event Hubs SDK to send a single event with a JSON payload to the configured event hub

prompt_templates.title: Batch Event Submission
prompt_templates.prompt: Write Rust code that creates an event batch, adds multiple events with metadata, checks batch capacity, and sends the batch to Azure Event Hubs

prompt_templates.title: Partition-Aware Consumer
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement a Rust consumer that opens receivers for all partitions, receives events from each partition concurrently, and prints event metadata

prompt_templates.title: Checkpointed Distributed Consumer
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a Rust application that uses ConsumerClient with Blob checkpoint store to track processing progress and enable failover recovery for distributed consumption

output_examples.input: Send a batch of sensor readings to Event Hubs
output_examples.output: Successfully sent batch with 50 events to partition 2. Batch size: 4096 bytes. Sequence numbers: 1001-1050

output_examples.input: Receive events from partition 0 with checkpointing
output_examples.output: Received 25 events from partition 0. Last sequence number: 2847. Checkpoint saved to blob storage. Processing time: 120ms

best_practices: Reuse ProducerClient and ConsumerClient instances instead of creating new ones for each operation
Use batch sends instead of individual events to improve throughput and reduce latency
Implement checkpointing with Blob storage when running distributed consumers for reliable recovery
anti_patterns: Creating new client instances for every event instead of reusing connections
Sending events individually in a loop without batching when throughput matters
Ignoring batch capacity limits which can cause event loss or send failures

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure Event Hubs with this Rust SDK?
faq.answer: Use the DeveloperToolsCredential from azure_identity crate for local development. For production, use ManagedIdentityCredential or ServicePrincipalCredential with appropriate Azure RBAC permissions.

faq.question: What is the maximum batch size for Event Hubs?
faq.answer: The maximum batch size depends on your Event Hubs tier. Basic tier allows 256 KB per batch, Standard allows 1 MB, and Premium allows up to 4 MB. Use try_add_event_data to check capacity before adding events.

faq.question: How do I handle partition rebalancing in distributed consumers?
faq.answer: Use the checkpoint store feature with azure_messaging_eventhubs_checkpointstore_blob. It coordinates partition ownership across consumer instances and enables automatic failover when instances go offline.

faq.question: Can I send events to a specific partition?
faq.answer: Yes, include a partition_key in the event options when calling send_event or add_event_data. Events with the same partition key always route to the same partition, enabling ordered processing.

faq.question: What happens if event send fails due to network issues?
faq.answer: The SDK returns a Result with an error variant. Implement retry logic with exponential backoff. Consider using transaction IDs for idempotent sends to avoid duplicates during retries.

faq.question: How do I monitor Event Hubs throughput and latency?
faq.answer: Use Azure Monitor metrics for Event Hubs namespace. Track IncomingMessages, OutgoingMessages, and Requests metrics. For application-level monitoring, instrument send and receive operations with tracing spans.
